```json
    {
      "actionCode": "H8D000",

        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Rule provides for consideration of H.J. Res. 25, H.R. 1156 and H.R. 1968. The resolution provides for consideration of H.J. Res. 25, H.R. 1156, and H.R. 1968 under a closed rule. Also, the resolution provides for one hour of general debate and one motion to recommit on each measure.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },

      },
      "text": "Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 211. (consideration: CR H1102-1124)",

    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H1L220",
      "actionDate": "2025-03-11",

      },

    },
    {
      "actionCode": "H1L210",
      "actionDate": "2025-03-11",
      "actionTime": "10:09:59",

        "code": "2",
        "name": "House floor actions"
      },
      "text": "Rules Committee Resolution H. Res. 211 Reported to House. Rule provides for consideration of H.J. Res. 25, H.R. 1156 and H.R. 1968. The resolution provides for consideration of H.J. Res. 25, H.R. 1156, and H.R. 1968 under a closed rule. Also, the resolution provides for one hour of general debate and one motion to recommit on each measure.",
      "type": "Floor"
    },
```

```json
        },
        {
          "actions": {
            "actions": {
              "item": [
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-03-14",
                  "recordedVotes": {
                    "recordedVote": {
                      "chamber": "Senate",
                      "congress": "119",
                      "date": "2025-03-14T21:32:43Z",
                      "rollNumber": "130",
                      "sessionNumber": "1",
                      "url": "https://www.senate.gov/legislative/LIS/roll_call_votes/vote1191/vote_119_1_00130.xml"
                    }
                  },
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1274, under the order of 3/14/2025, not having achieved 60 votes in the affirmative, was not agreed to in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 47 - 53. Record Vote Number: 130.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionDate": "2025-03-14",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "0",
                    "name": "Senate"
                  },
                  "text": "Amendment SA 1274 proposed by Senator Duckworth. (consideration: CR S1770-1771) To make veteran Federal employees who were involuntarily dismissed without cause eligible for reinstatement and to require reports from Executive agencies on the number of veteran employees fired from such agencies.",
                  "type": "Floor"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "Intro-S",
                  "actionDate": "2025-03-14",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",
                    "name": "Library of Congress"
                  },
                  "type": "IntroReferral"
                },
                {
                  "actionCode": "93000",
                  "actionDate": "2025-03-14",
                  "sourceSystem": {
                    "code": "9",

            "item": {

              "firstName": "Tammy",
              "fullName": "Sen. Duckworth, Tammy [D-IL]",

              "state": "IL",
              "type": "Proposed on behalf of"
            }
          },
          "proposedDate": "2025-03-14T04:00:00Z",
          "purpose": "To make veteran Federal employees who were involuntarily dismissed without cause eligible for reinstatement and to require reports from Executive agencies on the number of veteran employees fired from such agencies.",
          "sponsors": {
            "item": {
              "bioguideId": "D000622",

              "party": "D",
              "state": "IL"
            }
          },
```

## API Data: fullTexts

```json
{
  "fullTexts": [
    {
      "fetchError": null,
      "sourceFormat": "Formatted XML",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.govinfo.gov/bulkdata/BILLS/119/1/hr/BILLS-119hr1968ih.xml",
      "text": "I\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nH. R. 1968\nIN THE HOUSE OF REPRESENTATIVES\nMarch 10, 2025 Mr. Cole introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Appropriations , and in addition to the Committee on the Budget , for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned\nA BILL\nMaking further continuing appropriations and other extensions for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025, and for other purposes.\n#### 1. Short title\nThis Act may be cited as the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2025 .\n#### 2. Table of contents\nThe table of contents of this Act is as follows:\nSec. 1. Short title.\nSec. 2. Table of contents.\nSec. 3. References.\nDivision A\u2014Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025\nTitle I\u2014General Provisions\nTitle II\u2014Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies\nTitle III\u2014Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies\nTitle IV\u2014Department of Defense\nTitle V\u2014Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies\nTitle VI\u2014Financial Services and General Government\nTitle VII\u2014Department of Homeland Security\nTitle VIII\u2014Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies\nTitle IX\u2014Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies\nTitle X\u2014Legislative Branch\nTitle XI\u2014Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies\nTitle XII\u2014Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs\nTitle XIII\u2014Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies\nDivision B\u2014Health\nTitle I\u2014Public health extenders\nSec. 2101. Extension for community health centers, National Health Service Corps, and teaching health centers that operate GME programs.\nSec. 2102. Extension of special diabetes programs.\nSec. 2103. National health security extensions.\nTitle II\u2014Medicare\nSec. 2201. Extension of increased inpatient hospital payment adjustment for certain low-volume hospitals.\nSec. 2202. Extension of the Medicare-dependent hospital (MDH) program.\nSec. 2203. Extension of add-on payments for ambulance services.\nSec. 2204. Extension of funding for quality measure endorsement, input, and selection.\nSec. 2205. Extension of funding outreach and assistance for low-income programs.\nSec. 2206. Extension of the work geographic index floor.\nSec. 2207. Extension of certain telehealth flexibilities.\nSec. 2208. Extending acute hospital care at home waiver authorities.\nSec. 2209. Extension of temporary inclusion of authorized oral antiviral drugs as covered part D drugs.\nSec. 2210. Medicare improvement fund.\nSec. 2211. Medicare sequestration.\nTitle III\u2014Human services\nSec. 2301. Sexual risk avoidance education extension.\nSec. 2302. Personal responsibility education extension.\nSec. 2303. Extension of funding for family-to-family health information centers.\nTitle IV\u2014Medicaid\nSec. 2401. Delaying Medicaid DSH reductions.\nDivision C\u2014Other Matters\nSec. 3101. Commodity futures trading commission whistleblower program.\nSec. 3102. Protection of certain facilities and assets from unmanned aircraft.\nSec. 3103. Additional special assessment.\nSec. 3104. National cybersecurity protection system authorization.\nSec. 3105. Extension of temporary order for fentanyl-related substances.\nSec. 3106. Budgetary effects.\n#### 3. References\nExcept as expressly provided otherwise, any reference to this Act contained in any division of this Act shall be treated as referring only to the provisions of that division.\nA\nFull-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025\nThe following sums are hereby appropriated, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, and out of applicable corporate or other revenues, receipts, and funds, for the several departments, agencies, corporations, and other organizational units of Government for fiscal year 2025, and for other purposes, namely:\nI\nGeneral Provisions\n#### 1101.\n##### (a)\nSuch amounts as may be necessary, at the level specified in subsection (c) and under the authority and conditions provided in applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2024, for projects or activities (including the costs of direct loans and loan guarantees) that are not otherwise specifically provided for, and for which appropriations, funds, or other authority were made available in the following appropriations Acts:\n**(1)**\nThe Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division B of Public Law 118\u201342 ).\n**(2)**\nThe Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division C of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except section 510 shall be applied by substituting $1,900,000,000 for $1,353,000,000 , except section 521(a)(1) shall be applied by substituting $30,000,000 for $35,000,000 , except section 521(a)(4) shall be applied by substituting $9,560,000,000 for $12,440,000,000 , except section 521(b)(3) shall be applied by substituting $15,000,000 for $5,000,000 , except section 521(b)(4) shall be applied by substituting $125,000,000 for $120,000,000 , except section 521(b)(5) shall be applied by substituting $20,000,000 for $15,000,000 , except section 521(c)(1) shall be applied by substituting $300,000,000 for $131,572,000 , except section 521(c)(2) shall be applied by substituting $250,000,000 for $500,000,000 , except section 521(f) shall be applied by inserting or title II of division C of Public Law 118\u201342 after 117\u2013328 , and except sections 222, 521(a)(2), 521(a)(3), 521(a)(5), 521(b)(1), and 521(b)(2).\n**(3)**\nThe Department of Defense Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118\u201347 ).\n**(4)**\nThe Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division D of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except the third proviso under the heading Corps of Engineers\u2014Civil\u2014Construction , and except sections 307, 311, and 312.\n**(5)**\nThe Financial Services and General Government Appropriations Act, 2024 (division B of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except section 635 shall be applied by substituting $400,000,000 for $387,500,000 , except the last proviso under the heading Federal Payment for Defender Services in District of Columbia Courts shall be applied by substituting $12,000,000 for $25,000,000 , and except sections 636, 637, 638, and 639.\n**(6)**\nThe Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2024 (division C of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except sections 543 through 546, and including sections 102 through 105 of title I of division G of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n**(7)**\nThe Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division E of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except the fourth and fifth paragraphs under the heading National Park Service\u2014Administrative Provisions , except the eighteenth proviso under the first paragraph under the heading Environmental Protection Agency\u2014State and Tribal Assistance Grants , and except sections 446 through 448.\n**(8)**\nThe Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division D of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except section 240 shall be applied by substituting $1,471,000,000 for $1,250,000,000 and by substituting 2025, except that no amounts may be rescinded from amounts that were previously designated by the Congress as being for an emergency requirement pursuant to a concurrent resolution on the budget or the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 for 2024 in such section, except sections 241 and 310, except the amount included in section 528 shall be applied by substituting $13,059,000,000 for $14,224,000,000 , and except the amount included in section 529 shall be applied by substituting $160,000,000 for $4,309,000,000 .\n**(9)**\nThe Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2024 (division E of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except the matter under the heading Joint Items, Joint Congressional Committee on Inaugural Ceremonies of 2025 , and including section 7 in the matter preceding division A of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n**(10)**\nThe Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except the second provisos under the headings Veterans Health Administration, Medical Services , Veterans Health Administration, Medical Community Care , and Veterans Health Administration, Medical Support and Compliance .\n**(11)**\nThe Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except sections 7074(e) and 7075(a).\n**(12)**\nThe Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except sections 108, 109B, 119G, 125, 154, 165, 171, and 236.\n##### (b)\nFor purposes of this division, the term level means an amount.\n##### (c)\nThe level referred to in subsection (a) shall be the amounts appropriated in the appropriations Acts referred to in such subsection, including transfers and obligation limitations.\n#### 1102.\nAppropriations made by section 1101 shall be available to the extent and in the manner that would be provided by the pertinent appropriations Act.\n#### 1103.\nAppropriations provided by this division that, in the applicable appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, carried a multiple-year or no-year period of availability shall retain a comparable period of availability.\n#### 1104.\nNo appropriation or funds made available or authority granted pursuant to section 1101 shall be used to initiate or resume any project or activity for which appropriations, funds, or other authority were specifically prohibited during fiscal year 2024.\n#### 1105.\nExcept as otherwise expressly provided in this division, the requirements, authorities, conditions, limitations, and other provisions of the appropriations Acts referred to in section 1101 shall continue in effect through the date specified in section 1106.\n#### 1106.\nUnless otherwise provided for in this division or in the applicable appropriations Act, appropriations and funds made available and authority granted pursuant to this division shall be available through September 30, 2025.\n#### 1107.\nExpenditures made pursuant to the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 ( Public Law 118\u201383 ) shall be charged to the applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization provided by this division.\n#### 1108.\nFunds appropriated by this division may be obligated and expended notwithstanding section 10 of Public Law 91\u2013672 ( 22 U.S.C. 2412 ), section 15 of the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 ( 22 U.S.C. 2680 ), section 313 of the Foreign Relations Authorization Act, Fiscal Years 1994 and 1995 ( 22 U.S.C. 6212 ), and section 504(a)(1) of the National Security Act of 1947 ( 50 U.S.C. 3094(a)(1) ).\n#### 1109.\n##### (a)\nFor entitlements and other mandatory payments whose budget authority was provided in appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2024, and for activities under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, the levels established by section 1101 shall be the amounts necessary to maintain program levels under current law and under the authority and conditions provided in the applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2024.\n##### (b)\nIn addition to the amounts otherwise provided by section 1101, the following amounts shall be available for the following accounts for advance payments for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026:\n**(1)**\nDepartment of Labor\u2014Office of Workers\u2019 Compensation Programs\u2014Special Benefits for Disabled Coal Miners , for benefit payments under title IV of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977, $6,000,000, to remain available until expended.\n**(2)**\nDepartment of Health and Human Services\u2014Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services\u2014Grants to States for Medicaid , for payments to States or in the case of section 1928 on behalf of States under title XIX of the Social Security Act, $261,063,820,000, to remain available until expended.\n**(3)**\nDepartment of Health and Human Services\u2014Administration for Children and Families\u2014Payments to States for Child Support Enforcement and Family Support Programs , for payments to States or other non-Federal entities under titles I, IV\u2013D, X, XI, XIV, and XVI of the Social Security Act and the Act of July 5, 1960 (24 U.S.C. ch. 9), $1,600,000,000, to remain available until expended.\n**(4)**\nDepartment of Health and Human Services\u2014Administration for Children and Families\u2014Payments for Foster Care and Permanency , for payments to States or other non-Federal entities under title IV\u2013E of the Social Security Act, $3,600,000,000.\n**(5)**\nSocial Security Administration\u2014Supplemental Security Income Program , for benefit payments under title XVI of the Social Security Act, $22,100,000,000, to remain available until expended.\n#### 1110.\n##### (a)\nEach amount incorporated by reference in this Act that was previously designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 or as being for disaster relief pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(D) of such Act is designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of such Act or as being for disaster relief pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(D) of such Act, respectively.\n##### (b)\nSection 6 of Public Laws 118\u201342 and 118\u201347 and section 11206(4) of this Act shall apply to amounts designated in subsection (a).\n##### (c)\nEach amount incorporated by reference in this Act that was previously designated in division B of Public Law 117\u2013159 , division J of Public Law 117\u201358 , or in section 443(b) of division G of Public Law 117\u2013328 by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to a concurrent resolution on the budget shall continue to be treated as an amount specified in section 103(b) of division A of Public Law 118\u20135 .\n#### 1111.\nAny language specifying an earmark in an appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, or in a committee report or joint explanatory statement accompanying such an Act, shall have no legal effect with respect to funds appropriated by this division. For purposes of this section, the term earmark means a congressional earmark, community project funding, or congressionally directed spending item, as defined in clause 9(e) of rule XXI of the Rules of the House of Representatives and paragraph 5(a) of rule XLIV of the Standing Rules of the Senate.\n#### 1112.\nWith respect to any discretionary account for which advance appropriations were provided for fiscal year 2025 or 2026 in an appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, in addition to amounts otherwise made available by this division, advance appropriations are provided in the same amount for fiscal year 2026 or 2027, respectively, with a comparable period of availability.\n#### 1113.\n##### (a)\nNot later than 45 days after the date of the enactment of this division, each department and agency in subsection (c) shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate a spending, expenditure, or operating plan for fiscal year 2025\u2014\n**(1)**\nat the program, project, or activity level (or, for foreign assistance programs funded in the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, at the country, regional, and central program level, and for any international organization); or\n**(2)**\nas applicable, at any greater level of detail required for funds covered by such a plan in an appropriations Act referred to in section 1101, in the joint explanatory statement accompanying such Act, or in committee report language incorporated by reference in such joint explanatory statement.\n##### (b)\nIf a sequestration is ordered by the President under section 254 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the spending, expenditure, or operating plan required by this section shall reflect such sequestration.\n##### (c)\nThe departments and agencies to which this section applies are as follows:\n**(1)**\nThe Department of Agriculture.\n**(2)**\nThe Department of Commerce, including the United States Patent and Trademark Office.\n**(3)**\nThe Department of Defense, other than for amounts made available in section 1101(a)(3) and title IV of this division.\n**(4)**\nThe Department of Education.\n**(5)**\nThe Department of Energy.\n**(6)**\nThe Department of Health and Human Services.\n**(7)**\nThe Department of Homeland Security.\n**(8)**\nThe Department of Housing and Urban Development.\n**(9)**\nThe Department of the Interior.\n**(10)**\nThe Department of Justice.\n**(11)**\nThe Department of Labor.\n**(12)**\nThe Department of State and United States Agency for International Development.\n**(13)**\nThe Department of Transportation.\n**(14)**\nThe Department of the Treasury.\n**(15)**\nThe Department of Veterans Affairs.\n**(16)**\nThe National Aeronautics and Space Administration.\n**(17)**\nThe National Science Foundation.\n**(18)**\nThe Judiciary.\n**(19)**\nWith respect to amounts made available under the heading Executive Office of the President and Funds Appropriated to the President , agencies funded under such heading.\n**(20)**\nThe Federal Communications Commission.\n**(21)**\nThe General Services Administration.\n**(22)**\nThe Office of Personnel Management.\n**(23)**\nThe National Archives and Records Administration.\n**(24)**\nThe Securities and Exchange Commission.\n**(25)**\nThe Small Business Administration.\n**(26)**\nThe Environmental Protection Agency.\n**(27)**\nThe Indian Health Service.\n**(28)**\nThe Smithsonian Institution.\n**(29)**\nThe Social Security Administration.\n**(30)**\nThe Corporation for National and Community Service.\n**(31)**\nThe Corporation for Public Broadcasting.\n**(32)**\nThe Food and Drug Administration.\n**(33)**\nThe Commodity Futures Trading Commission.\n**(34)**\nThe United States International Development Finance Corporation.\n**(35)**\nThe Architect of the Capitol.\n#### 1114.\nNot later than May 15, 2025, and each month thereafter through November 1, 2025, the Office of Management and Budget shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate a report on all obligations incurred in fiscal year 2025, by each department and agency, using funds made available by this division. Such report shall\u2014\n**(1)**\nset forth obligations by account; and\n**(2)**\ncompare the obligations incurred in the period covered by the report to the obligations incurred in the same period in fiscal year 2024.\n#### 1115.\nDuring the period covered by this Act, section 235(b) of the Sentencing Reform Act of 1984 ( 18 U.S.C. 3551 note; Public Law 98\u2013473 ; 98 Stat. 2032), as such section relates to chapter 311 of title 18, United States Code, and the United States Parole Commission, shall be applied by substituting 37 for 36 each place it appears.\n#### 1116.\nAny amount appropriated by this Act, designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, and subsequently so designated by the President, and transferred pursuant to transfer authorities provided by this division shall retain such designation.\nII\nAgriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies\n#### 1201.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, the level for each of the following accounts shall be as follows:\n**(1)**\n$0 for Department of 118\u201342 is amended by striking paragraph (1).\n(Including Transfers of Funds)\n#### 1205.\nNotwithstanding section 1101, amounts made available under the heading Agricultural Programs, Farm ).\n(Including Transfers of Funds)\n#### 1206.\nNotwithstanding section 1101, amounts may be transferred among accounts of the Department of Agriculture under the heading Rural Development Programs to allow for the program levels to be equal to, to the maximum extent practicable, the levels enacted for fiscal year 2024: Provided , That $34,000,000 shall be transferred from such accounts to Rural Development Programs, Rural Housing Service, Rental Assistance Program .\n#### 1207.\nTitle I of division N of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 ( Public Law 117\u2013328 ), is amended in the last proviso under the heading Agricultural Programs\u2014Processing, Research and Marketing\u2014Office of the Secretary , by adding at the end the following: , except that the Secretary shall allow producers to retain payments not to exceed 90 percent of the producer\u2019s revenue losses (as determined by the Secretary) if the Secretary determines a de minimis amount, as defined by the Secretary, of a producer\u2019s revenue loss is attributable to crops for which the producer did not insure or obtain coverage under the Noninsured Crop Disaster Assistance Program under section 196 of the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 ( 7 U.S.C. 7333 ): Provided further , That amounts repurposed pursuant to this section that were previously designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to a concurrent resolution on the budget are designated as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985. .\nIII\nCommerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies\n#### 1301.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, the level for the following accounts shall be as follows:\n##### that follows before the period and inserting CH\u201353K Heavy Lift helicopters, T408 engines, and USS Virginia Class (SSN\u2013774) .\n#### 1419.\nDuring the period covered by this Act, section 8092 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 shall be applied by substituting $204,939,000 for $142,008,000 .\n#### 1420.\nFor an additional amount there is appropriated to the Department of Defense Credit Program Account established pursuant to section 149(e)(5) of title 10, United States Code, as amended by section 905(a) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025 ( Public Law 118\u2013159 ), $89,049,000, to remain available until expended, to carry out a pilot program on capital assistance to support defense investment in the industrial base as authorized by section 149(e) of such title, of which up to $7,900,000 may be used for administrative expenses and project-specific transaction costs: Provided , That costs of loans and loan guarantees, including the cost of modifying such loans and loan guarantees, shall be as defined in section 502 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974: Provided further, That such amounts are available to subsidize gross obligations for the principal amount of loans, and total loan principal, any part of which is to be guaranteed, not to exceed $4,000,000,000: Provided further, That, for the purposes of carrying out the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, the Director of the Congressional Budget Office may request, and the Secretary shall promptly provide documentation and information relating to a project receiving capital assistance as authorized under section 149(e) of such title: Provided further, That section 8140 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 shall not apply to funds appropriated by this Act.\n(Including Transfer of Funds)\n#### 1421.\nFor an additional amount for the Department of Defense, $8,000,000,000, to remain available until September 30, 2025, for transfer to military personnel accounts, operation and maintenance accounts, and the Defense Working Capital Funds, in addition to amounts otherwise made available only for U.S. military operations, force protection, and deterrence led by Commander, United States Central Command and Commander, United States European Command: Provided , That none of the funds provided under this section may be obligated or expended until 30 days after the Secretary of Defense provides to the congressional defense committees an execution plan: Provided further, That not less than 15 days prior to any transfer of funds, the Secretary of Defense shall notify the congressional defense committees of the details of any such transfer: Provided further , That the transfer authority provided under this section is in addition to any other transfer authority provided elsewhere in this Act: Provided further , That upon transfer, the funds shall be merged with and available for the same purposes, and for the same time period, as the appropriation to which transferred: Provided further, That upon a determination that all or part of the funds transferred from this appropriation are not necessary for the purposes provided herein, such amounts may be transferred back and merged with this appropriation.\n#### 1422.\n##### (a)\nNot later than 45 days after the date of the enactment of this division, the Department of Defense, after consultation with the Subcommittees on Defense of the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate, shall submit to such Subcommittees a spending, expenditure, or operating plan for fiscal year 2025 for appropriations or funds made available pursuant to section 1101(a)(3) or any other provision of this title for the Department of Defense at the same level of detail required for the report outlined by section 8007 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n##### (b)\nNo program, project, or activity may be included in the expenditure plan submitted pursuant to subsection (a) unless such program, project, or activity was provided for in H.R. 8774 (as engrossed in the House of Representatives on June 28, 2024) or S. 4921 (as reported by the Senate Committee on Appropriations on August 1, 2024) or in the reports accompanying those Acts.\n##### (c)\nThe plan submitted pursuant to subsection (a) shall serve as the baseline for reprogramming and transfer authorities for fiscal year 2025 under the authorities and conditions of sections 8005 and 8006 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n##### (d)\nIf a sequestration is ordered by the President under section 254 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the spending, expenditure, or operating plan required by this section shall reflect such sequestration.\nV\nEnergy and Water Development and Related Agencies\n#### 1501.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, the level for the following accounts shall be applied as follows:\n##### Senate a detailed work plan for fiscal year 2025 with respect to the funds appropriated by this division for Corps of Engineers\u2014Civil : Provided , That specific studies and projects shall not be eligible to receive such funds made available under the headings Investigations , Construction , and Mississippi River and Tributaries , as applicable, unless such studies and projects are active as of the date that is the day prior to the date of enactment of this division and are otherwise eligible to receive funds made available under such headings: Provided further , That the Assistant Secretary of the Army for Civil Works shall not deviate from the work plan submitted pursuant to this subsection once the plan has been submitted to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate, except as provided in section 101 of division D of Public Law 118\u201342 .\n#### 1504.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, language under the heading Department of Energy\u2014Energy Programs\u2014Uranium Enrichment Decontamination and Decommissioning Fund in Public Law 118\u201342 shall be applied to funds appropriated by this Act by substituting to be Extension of increased inpatient hospital payment adjustment for certain low-volume hospitals\n##### (a) In general\nSection 1886(d)(12) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395ww(d)(12) ) is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin subparagraph (B), by striking during the portion of fiscal year 2025 beginning on April 1, 2025, and ending on September 30, 2025, and ;\n**(2)**\nin subparagraph (C)(i)\u2014\n**(A)**\nin the matter preceding subclause (I)\u2014\n**(i)**\nby striking or portion of a fiscal year ; and\n**(ii)**\nby striking 2024 and the portion of fiscal year 2025 beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting 2025 ;\n**(B)**\nin subclause (III), by striking 2024 and the portion of fiscal year 2025 beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting 2025 ; and\n**(C)**\nin subclause (IV), by striking the portion of fiscal year 2025 beginning on April 1, 2025, and ending on September 30, 2025, and ; and\n**(3)**\nin subparagraph (D)\u2014\n**(A)**\nin the matter preceding clause (i), by striking 2024 or during the portion of fiscal year 2025 beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting 2025 ; and\n**(B)**\nin clause (ii), by striking 2024 and the portion of fiscal year 2025 beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting 2025 .\n##### (b) Implementation\nNotwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Health and Human Services may implement the amendments made by this section by program instruction or otherwise.\n#### 2202. Extension of temporary order for fentanyl-related substances\nEffective as if included in the enactment of the Temporary Reauthorization and Study of the Emergency Scheduling of Fentanyl Analogues Act ( Public Law 116\u2013114 ), section 2 of such Act is amended by striking March 14, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 3106. Budgetary effects\n##### (a) Statutory PAYGO scorecards\nThe budgetary effects of divisions B and C shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.\n##### (b) Senate PAYGO scorecards\nThe budgetary effects of divisions B and C shall not be entered on any PAYGO scorecard maintained for purposes of section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress).\n##### (c) Classification of budgetary effects\nNotwithstanding Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines set forth in the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference accompanying Conference Report 105\u2013217 and section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the budgetary effects of divisions B and C shall not be estimated\u2014\n**(1)**\nfor purposes of section 251 of such Act;\n**(2)**\nfor purposes of an allocation to the Committee on Appropriations pursuant to section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974; and\n**(3)**\nfor purposes of paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 as being included in an appropriation Act.",

      "text": "II\nCalendar No. 26\n119th CONGRESS\n1st Session\nH. R. 1968\nIN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES\nMarch 11 (legislative day, March 10), 2025 Received; read the first time\nMarch 11, 2025 Read the second time and placed on the calendar\nAN ACT\nMaking further continuing appropriations and other extensions for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025, and for other purposes.\n#### 1. Short title\nThis Act may be cited as the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2025 .\n#### 2. Table of contents\nThe table of contents of this Act is as follows:\nSec. 1. Short title.\nSec. 2. Table of contents.\nSec. 3. References.\nDivision A\u2014Full-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025\nTitle I\u2014General Provisions\nTitle II\u2014Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies\nTitle III\u2014Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies\nTitle IV\u2014Department of Defense\nTitle V\u2014Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies\nTitle VI\u2014Financial Services and General Government\nTitle VII\u2014Department of Homeland Security\nTitle VIII\u2014Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies\nTitle IX\u2014Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies\nTitle X\u2014Legislative Branch\nTitle XI\u2014Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies\nTitle XII\u2014Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs\nTitle XIII\u2014Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies\nDivision B\u2014Health\nTitle I\u2014Public health extenders\nSec. 2101. Extension for community health centers, National Health Service Corps, and teaching health centers that operate GME programs.\nSec. 2102. Extension of special diabetes programs.\nSec. 2103. National health security extensions.\nTitle II\u2014Medicare\nSec. 2201. Extension of increased inpatient hospital payment adjustment for certain low-volume hospitals.\nSec. 2202. Extension of the Medicare-dependent hospital (MDH) program.\nSec. 2203. Extension of add-on payments for ambulance services.\nSec. 2204. Extension of funding for quality measure endorsement, input, and selection.\nSec. 2205. Extension of funding outreach and assistance for low-income programs.\nSec. 2206. Extension of the work geographic index floor.\nSec. 2207. Extension of certain telehealth flexibilities.\nSec. 2208. Extending acute hospital care at home waiver authorities.\nSec. 2209. Extension of temporary inclusion of authorized oral antiviral drugs as covered part D drugs.\nSec. 2210. Medicare improvement fund.\nSec. 2211. Medicare sequestration.\nTitle III\u2014Human services\nSec. 2301. Sexual risk avoidance education extension.\nSec. 2302. Personal responsibility education extension.\nSec. 2303. Extension of funding for family-to-family health information centers.\nTitle IV\u2014Medicaid\nSec. 2401. Delaying Medicaid DSH reductions.\nDivision C\u2014Other Matters\nSec. 3101. Commodity futures trading commission whistleblower program.\nSec. 3102. Protection of certain facilities and assets from unmanned aircraft.\nSec. 3103. Additional special assessment.\nSec. 3104. National cybersecurity protection system authorization.\nSec. 3105. Extension of temporary order for fentanyl-related substances.\nSec. 3106. Budgetary effects.\n#### 3. References\nExcept as expressly provided otherwise, any reference to this Act contained in any division of this Act shall be treated as referring only to the provisions of that division.\nA\nFull-Year Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025\nThe following sums are hereby appropriated, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, and out of applicable corporate or other revenues, receipts, and funds, for the several departments, agencies, corporations, and other organizational units of Government for fiscal year 2025, and for other purposes, namely:\nI\nGeneral Provisions\n#### 1101.\n##### (a)\nSuch amounts as may be necessary, at the level specified in subsection (c) and under the authority and conditions provided in applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2024, for projects or activities (including the costs of direct loans and loan guarantees) that are not otherwise specifically provided for, and for which appropriations, funds, or other authority were made available in the following appropriations Acts:\n**(1)**\nThe Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division B of Public Law 118\u201342 ).\n**(2)**\nThe Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division C of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except section 510 shall be applied by substituting $1,900,000,000 for $1,353,000,000 , except section 521(a)(1) shall be applied by substituting $30,000,000 for $35,000,000 , except section 521(a)(4) shall be applied by substituting $9,560,000,000 for $12,440,000,000 , except section 521(b)(3) shall be applied by substituting $15,000,000 for $5,000,000 , except section 521(b)(4) shall be applied by substituting $125,000,000 for $120,000,000 , except section 521(b)(5) shall be applied by substituting $20,000,000 for $15,000,000 , except section 521(c)(1) shall be applied by substituting $300,000,000 for $131,572,000 , except section 521(c)(2) shall be applied by substituting $250,000,000 for $500,000,000 , except section 521(f) shall be applied by inserting or title II of division C of Public Law 118\u201342 after 117\u2013328 , and except sections 222, 521(a)(2), 521(a)(3), 521(a)(5), 521(b)(1), and 521(b)(2).\n**(3)**\nThe Department of Defense Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118\u201347 ).\n**(4)**\nThe Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division D of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except the third proviso under the heading Corps of Engineers\u2014Civil\u2014Construction , and except sections 307, 311, and 312.\n**(5)**\nThe Financial Services and General Government Appropriations Act, 2024 (division B of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except section 635 shall be applied by substituting $400,000,000 for $387,500,000 , except the last proviso under the heading Federal Payment for Defender Services in District of Columbia Courts shall be applied by substituting $12,000,000 for $25,000,000 , and except sections 636, 637, 638, and 639.\n**(6)**\nThe Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Act, 2024 (division C of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except sections 543 through 546, and including sections 102 through 105 of title I of division G of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n**(7)**\nThe Department of the Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division E of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except the fourth and fifth paragraphs under the heading National Park Service\u2014Administrative Provisions , except the eighteenth proviso under the first paragraph under the heading Environmental Protection Agency\u2014State and Tribal Assistance Grants , and except sections 446 through 448.\n**(8)**\nThe Departments of Labor, Health and Human Services, and Education, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division D of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except section 240 shall be applied by substituting $1,471,000,000 for $1,250,000,000 and by substituting 2025, except that no amounts may be rescinded from amounts that were previously designated by the Congress as being for an emergency requirement pursuant to a concurrent resolution on the budget or the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 for 2024 in such section, except sections 241 and 310, except the amount included in section 528 shall be applied by substituting $13,059,000,000 for $14,224,000,000 , and except the amount included in section 529 shall be applied by substituting $160,000,000 for $4,309,000,000 .\n**(9)**\nThe Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 2024 (division E of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except the matter under the heading Joint Items, Joint Congressional Committee on Inaugural Ceremonies of 2025 , and including section 7 in the matter preceding division A of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n**(10)**\nThe Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except the second provisos under the headings Veterans Health Administration, Medical Services , Veterans Health Administration, Medical Community Care , and Veterans Health Administration, Medical Support and Compliance .\n**(11)**\nThe Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201347 ), except sections 7074(e) and 7075(a).\n**(12)**\nThe Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2024 (division F of Public Law 118\u201342 ), except sections 108, 109B, 119G, 125, 154, 165, 171, and 236.\n##### (b)\nFor purposes of this division, the term level means an amount.\n##### (c)\nThe level referred to in subsection (a) shall be the amounts appropriated in the appropriations Acts referred to in such subsection, including transfers and obligation limitations.\n#### 1102.\nAppropriations made by section 1101 shall be available to the extent and in the manner that would be provided by the pertinent appropriations Act.\n#### 1103.\nAppropriations provided by this division that, in the applicable appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, carried a multiple-year or no-year period of availability shall retain a comparable period of availability.\n#### 1104.\nNo appropriation or funds made available or authority granted pursuant to section 1101 shall be used to initiate or resume any project or activity for which appropriations, funds, or other authority were specifically prohibited during fiscal year 2024.\n#### 1105.\nExcept as otherwise expressly provided in this division, the requirements, authorities, conditions, limitations, and other provisions of the appropriations Acts referred to in section 1101 shall continue in effect through the date specified in section 1106.\n#### 1106.\nUnless otherwise provided for in this division or in the applicable appropriations Act, appropriations and funds made available and authority granted pursuant to this division shall be available through September 30, 2025.\n#### 1107.\nExpenditures made pursuant to the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2025 ( Public Law 118\u201383 ) shall be charged to the applicable appropriation, fund, or authorization provided by this division.\n#### 1108.\nFunds appropriated by this division may be obligated and expended notwithstanding section 10 of Public Law 91\u2013672 ( 22 U.S.C. 2412 ), section 15 of the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 ( 22 U.S.C. 2680 ), section 313 of the Foreign Relations Authorization Act, Fiscal Years 1994 and 1995 ( 22 U.S.C. 6212 ), and section 504(a)(1) of the National Security Act of 1947 ( 50 U.S.C. 3094(a)(1) ).\n#### 1109.\n##### (a)\nFor entitlements and other mandatory payments whose budget authority was provided in appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2024, and for activities under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, the levels established by section 1101 shall be the amounts necessary to maintain program levels under current law and under the authority and conditions provided in the applicable appropriations Acts for fiscal year 2024.\n##### (b)\nIn addition to the amounts otherwise provided by section 1101, the following amounts shall be available for the following accounts for advance payments for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026:\n**(1)**\nDepartment of Labor\u2014Office of Workers\u2019 Compensation Programs\u2014Special Benefits for Disabled Coal Miners , for benefit payments under title IV of the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977, $6,000,000, to remain available until expended.\n**(2)**\nDepartment of Health and Human Services\u2014Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services\u2014Grants to States for Medicaid , for payments to States or in the case of section 1928 on behalf of States under title XIX of the Social Security Act, $261,063,820,000, to remain available until expended.\n**(3)**\nDepartment of Health and Human Services\u2014Administration for Children and Families\u2014Payments to States for Child Support Enforcement and Family Support Programs , for payments to States or other non-Federal entities under titles I, IV\u2013D, X, XI, XIV, and XVI of the Social Security Act and the Act of July 5, 1960 (24 U.S.C. ch. 9), $1,600,000,000, to remain available until expended.\n**(4)**\nDepartment of Health and Human Services\u2014Administration for Children and Families\u2014Payments for Foster Care and Permanency , for payments to States or other non-Federal entities under title IV\u2013E of the Social Security Act, $3,600,000,000.\n**(5)**\nSocial Security Administration\u2014Supplemental Security Income Program , for benefit payments under title XVI of the Social Security Act, $22,100,000,000, to remain available until expended.\n#### 1110.\n##### (a)\nEach amount incorporated by reference in this Act that was previously designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 or as being for disaster relief pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(D) of such Act is designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of such Act or as being for disaster relief pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(D) of such Act, respectively.\n##### (b)\nSection 6 of Public Laws 118\u201342 and 118\u201347 and section 11206(4) of this Act shall apply to amounts designated in subsection (a).\n##### (c)\nEach amount incorporated by reference in this Act that was previously designated in division B of Public Law 117\u2013159 , division J of Public Law 117\u201358 , or in section 443(b) of division G of Public Law 117\u2013328 by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to a concurrent resolution on the budget shall continue to be treated as an amount specified in section 103(b) of division A of Public Law 118\u20135 .\n#### 1111.\nAny language specifying an earmark in an appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, or in a committee report or joint explanatory statement accompanying such an Act, shall have no legal effect with respect to funds appropriated by this division. For purposes of this section, the term earmark means a congressional earmark, community project funding, or congressionally directed spending item, as defined in clause 9(e) of rule XXI of the Rules of the House of Representatives and paragraph 5(a) of rule XLIV of the Standing Rules of the Senate.\n#### 1112.\nWith respect to any discretionary account for which advance appropriations were provided for fiscal year 2025 or 2026 in an appropriations Act for fiscal year 2024, in addition to amounts otherwise made available by this division, advance appropriations are provided in the same amount for fiscal year 2026 or 2027, respectively, with a comparable period of availability.\n#### 1113.\n##### (a)\nNot later than 45 days after the date of the enactment of this division, each department and agency in subsection (c) shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate a spending, expenditure, or operating plan for fiscal year 2025\u2014\n**(1)**\nat the program, project, or activity level (or, for foreign assistance programs funded in the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Appropriations Act, at the country, regional, and central program level, and for any international organization); or\n**(2)**\nas applicable, at any greater level of detail required for funds covered by such a plan in an appropriations Act referred to in section 1101, in the joint explanatory statement accompanying such Act, or in committee report language incorporated by reference in such joint explanatory statement.\n##### (b)\nIf a sequestration is ordered by the President under section 254 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the spending, expenditure, or operating plan required by this section shall reflect such sequestration.\n##### (c)\nThe departments and agencies to which this section applies are as follows:\n**(1)**\nThe Department of Agriculture.\n**(2)**\nThe Department of Commerce, including the United States Patent and Trademark Office.\n**(3)**\nThe Department of Defense, other than for amounts made available in section 1101(a)(3) and title IV of this division.\n**(4)**\nThe Department of Education.\n**(5)**\nThe Department of Energy.\n**(6)**\nThe Department of Health and Human Services.\n**(7)**\nThe Department of Homeland Security.\n**(8)**\nThe Department of Housing and Urban Development.\n**(9)**\nThe Department of the Interior.\n**(10)**\nThe Department of Justice.\n**(11)**\nThe Department of Labor.\n**(12)**\nThe Department of State and United States Agency for International Development.\n**(13)**\nThe Department of Transportation.\n**(14)**\nThe Department of the Treasury.\n**(15)**\nThe Department of Veterans Affairs.\n**(16)**\nThe National Aeronautics and Space Administration.\n**(17)**\nThe National Science Foundation.\n**(18)**\nThe Judiciary.\n**(19)**\nWith respect to amounts made available under the heading Executive Office of the President and Funds Appropriated to the President , agencies funded under such heading.\n**(20)**\nThe Federal Communications Commission.\n**(21)**\nThe General Services Administration.\n**(22)**\nThe Office of Personnel Management.\n**(23)**\nThe National Archives and Records Administration.\n**(24)**\nThe Securities and Exchange Commission.\n**(25)**\nThe Small Business Administration.\n**(26)**\nThe Environmental Protection Agency.\n**(27)**\nThe Indian Health Service.\n**(28)**\nThe Smithsonian Institution.\n**(29)**\nThe Social Security Administration.\n**(30)**\nThe Corporation for National and Community Service.\n**(31)**\nThe Corporation for Public Broadcasting.\n**(32)**\nThe Food and Drug Administration.\n**(33)**\nThe Commodity Futures Trading Commission.\n**(34)**\nThe United States International Development Finance Corporation.\n**(35)**\nThe Architect of the Capitol.\n#### 1114.\nNot later than May 15, 2025, and each month thereafter through November 1, 2025, the Office of Management and Budget shall submit to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate a report on all obligations incurred in fiscal year 2025, by each department and agency, using funds made available by this division. Such report shall\u2014\n**(1)**\nset forth obligations by account; and\n**(2)**\ncompare the obligations incurred in the period covered by the report to the obligations incurred in the same period in fiscal year 2024.\n#### 1115.\nDuring the period covered by this Act, section 235(b) of the Sentencing Reform Act of 1984 ( 18 U.S.C. 3551 note; Public Law 98\u2013473 ; 98 Stat. 2032), as such section relates to chapter 311 of title 18, United States Code, and the United States Parole Commission, shall be applied by substituting 37 for 36 each place it appears.\n#### 1116.\nAny amount appropriated by this Act, designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, and subsequently so designated by the President, and transferred pursuant to transfer authorities provided by this division shall retain such designation.\nII\nAgriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies\n#### 1201.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, the level for each of the following accounts shall be as follows:\n**(1)**\n$0 for Department of of the Livestock Mandatory Reporting Act of 1999 ( 7 U.S.C. 1635 note; Public Law 106\u201378 ) is amended by striking 2024 and inserting 2025 .\n#### 1204.\nSection 778 of division B of Public Law 118\u201342 is amended by striking paragraph (1).\n(Including Transfers of Funds)\n#### 1205.\nNotwithstanding section 1101, amounts made available under the heading Agricultural Programs, Farm Service Agency, Agricultural Credit Insurance Fund Program Account may be reprogrammed as necessary between allocations for loan categories to ensure that overall program levels are equal to, to the maximum extent practicable, the fiscal year 2024 program levels, notwithstanding section 346(b)(2)(A)(i)(I) of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act ( 7 U.S.C. 1994(b)(2)(A)(i)(I) ).\n(Including Transfers of Funds)\n#### 1206.\nNotwithstanding section 1101, amounts may be transferred among accounts of the Department of Agriculture under the heading Rural Development Programs to allow for the program levels to be equal to, to the maximum extent practicable, the levels enacted for fiscal year 2024: Provided , That $34,000,000 shall be transferred from such accounts to Rural Development Programs, Rural Housing Service, Rental Assistance Program .\n#### 1207.\nTitle I of division N of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023 ( Public Law 117\u2013328 ), is amended in the last proviso under the heading Agricultural Programs\u2014Processing, Research and Marketing\u2014Office of the Secretary , by adding at the end the following: , except that the Secretary shall allow producers to retain payments not to exceed 90 percent of the producer\u2019s revenue losses (as determined by the Secretary) if the Secretary determines a de minimis amount, as defined by the Secretary, of a producer\u2019s revenue loss is attributable to crops for which the producer did not insure or obtain coverage under the Noninsured Crop Disaster Assistance Program under section 196 of the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 ( 7 U.S.C. 7333 ): Provided further , That amounts repurposed pursuant to this section that were previously designated by the Congress as an emergency requirement pursuant to a concurrent resolution on the budget are designated as an emergency requirement pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985. .\nIII\nCommerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies\n#### 1301.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, the level for the following accounts shall be as follows:\n##### , 2024/2025: FFG\u2013Frigate Program, $98,759,000.\n#### 1418.\nThe last paragraph in section 8010 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 shall be applied by striking Naval Strike Missile and all that follows before the period and inserting CH\u201353K Heavy Lift helicopters, T408 engines, and USS Virginia Class (SSN\u2013774) .\n#### 1419.\nDuring the period covered by this Act, section 8092 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 shall be applied by substituting $204,939,000 for $142,008,000 .\n#### 1420.\nFor an additional amount there is appropriated to the Department of Defense Credit Program Account established pursuant to section 149(e)(5) of title 10, United States Code, as amended by section 905(a) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2025 ( Public Law 118\u2013159 ), $89,049,000, to remain available until expended, to carry out a pilot program on capital assistance to support defense investment in the industrial base as authorized by section 149(e) of such title, of which up to $7,900,000 may be used for administrative expenses and project-specific transaction costs: Provided , That costs of loans and loan guarantees, including the cost of modifying such loans and loan guarantees, shall be as defined in section 502 of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974: Provided further, That such amounts are available to subsidize gross obligations for the principal amount of loans, and total loan principal, any part of which is to be guaranteed, not to exceed $4,000,000,000: Provided further, That, for the purposes of carrying out the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, the Director of the Congressional Budget Office may request, and the Secretary shall promptly provide documentation and information relating to a project receiving capital assistance as authorized under section 149(e) of such title: Provided further, That section 8140 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 shall not apply to funds appropriated by this Act.\n(Including Transfer of Funds)\n#### 1421.\nFor an additional amount for the Department of Defense, $8,000,000,000, to remain available until September 30, 2025, for transfer to military personnel accounts, operation and maintenance accounts, and the Defense Working Capital Funds, in addition to amounts otherwise made available only for U.S. military operations, force protection, and deterrence led by Commander, United States Central Command and Commander, United States European Command: Provided , That none of the funds provided under this section may be obligated or expended until 30 days after the Secretary of Defense provides to the congressional defense committees an execution plan: Provided further, That not less than 15 days prior to any transfer of funds, the Secretary of Defense shall notify the congressional defense committees of the details of any such transfer: Provided further , That the transfer authority provided under this section is in addition to any other transfer authority provided elsewhere in this Act: Provided further , That upon transfer, the funds shall be merged with and available for the same purposes, and for the same time period, as the appropriation to which transferred: Provided further, That upon a determination that all or part of the funds transferred from this appropriation are not necessary for the purposes provided herein, such amounts may be transferred back and merged with this appropriation.\n#### 1422.\n##### (a)\nNot later than 45 days after the date of the enactment of this division, the Department of Defense, after consultation with the Subcommittees on Defense of the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate, shall submit to such Subcommittees a spending, expenditure, or operating plan for fiscal year 2025 for appropriations or funds made available pursuant to section 1101(a)(3) or any other provision of this title for the Department of Defense at the same level of detail required for the report outlined by section 8007 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n##### (b)\nNo program, project, or activity may be included in the expenditure plan submitted pursuant to subsection (a) unless such program, project, or activity was provided for in H.R. 8774 (as engrossed in the House of Representatives on June 28, 2024) or S. 4921 (as reported by the Senate Committee on Appropriations on August 1, 2024) or in the reports accompanying those Acts.\n##### (c)\nThe plan submitted pursuant to subsection (a) shall serve as the baseline for reprogramming and transfer authorities for fiscal year 2025 under the authorities and conditions of sections 8005 and 8006 of division A of Public Law 118\u201347 .\n##### (d)\nIf a sequestration is ordered by the President under section 254 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the spending, expenditure, or operating plan required by this section shall reflect such sequestration.\nV\nEnergy and Water Development and Related Agencies\n#### 1501.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, the level for the following accounts shall be applied as follows:\n##### eligible to receive such funds made available under the headings Investigations , Construction , and Mississippi River and Tributaries , as applicable, unless such studies and projects are active as of the date that is the day prior to the date of enactment of this division and are otherwise eligible to receive funds made available under such headings: Provided further , That the Assistant Secretary of the Army for Civil Works shall not deviate from the work plan submitted pursuant to this subsection once the plan has been submitted to the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate, except as provided in section 101 of division D of Public Law 118\u201342 .\n#### 1504.\nNotwithstanding section 1101 of this Act, language under the heading Department Extension of funding outreach and assistance for low-income programs\n##### (a) State health insurance assistance programs\nSubsection (a)(1)(B)(xiv) of section 119 of the Medicare Improvements for Patients and Providers Act of 2008 ( 42 U.S.C. 1395b\u20133 note) is amended by striking March 31, 2025, $22,500,000 and inserting September 30, 2025, $30,000,000 .\n##### (b) Area agencies on aging\nSubsection (b)(1)(B)(xiv) of such section 119 is amended by striking March 31, 2025, $22,500,000 and inserting September 30, 2025, $30,000,000 .\n##### (c) Aging and disability resource centers\nSubsection (c)(1)(B)(xiv) of such section 119 is amended by striking March 31, 2025, $8,500,000 and inserting September 30, 2025, $10,000,000 .\n##### (d) Coordination of efforts to inform older Americans about benefits available under Federal and State programs\nSubsection (d)(2)(xiv) of such section 119 is amended by striking March 31, 2025, $22,500,000 and inserting September 30, 2025, $30,000,000 .\n#### 2206. Extension of the work geographic index floor\nSection 1848(e)(1)(E) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395w\u20134(e)(1)(E) ) is amended by striking April 1, 2025 and inserting October 1, 2025 .\n#### 2207. U.S.C. 1395m(m) ) is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin paragraph (2)(B)(iii), by striking ending March 31, 2025 and inserting ending September 30, 2025 ; and\n**(2)**\nin paragraph (4)(C)(iii), by striking rural health clinics\nSection 1834(m)(8)(A) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395m(m)(8)(A) ) is amended by striking ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting ending on September 30, 2025 .\n##### (d) Delaying the in-Person requirements under Medicare for mental health services furnished through telehealth and telecommunications technology\n**(1) Delay in requirements for mental health services furnished through telehealth**\nSection 1834(m)(7)(B)(i) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395m(m)(7)(B)(i) ) is amended, in the matter preceding subclause (I), by striking on or after April 1, 2025 and inserting on or after October 1, 2025, .\n**(2) Mental health visits furnished by rural health clinics**\nSection 1834(y)(2) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395m(y)(2) ) is amended by striking April 1, 2025 and inserting October 1, 2025 .\n**(3) Mental health visits furnished by Federally qualified health centers**\nSection 1834(o)(4)(B) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395m(o)(4)(B) ) is amended by striking April 1, 2025 and inserting October 1, 2025 .\n##### (e) Allowing for the furnishing of audio-only telehealth services\nSection 1834(m)(9) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395m(m)(9) ) is amended by striking ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting ending on September 30, 2025 .\n##### (f) Extending use of telehealth To conduct face-to-Face encounter prior to recertification of eligibility for hospice care\nSection 1814(a)(7)(D)(i)(II) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395f(a)(7)(D)(i)(II) ) is amended by striking ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting ending on September 30, 2025 .\n##### (g) Program instruction authority\nThe Secretary of Health and Human Services may implement the amendments made by this section through program instruction or otherwise.\n#### 2208. Extending acute hospital care at home waiver authorities\nSection 1866G(a)(1) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395cc\u20137(a)(1) ) is amended by striking March 31, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 2209. Extension of temporary inclusion of authorized oral antiviral drugs as covered part D drugs\nSection 1860D\u20132(e)(1)(C) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395w\u2013102(e)(1)(C) ) is amended by striking March 31, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 2210. Medicare improvement fund\nSection 1898(b)(1) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1395iii(b)(1) ) is amended by striking $1,251,000,000 and inserting $1,804,000,000 .\n#### 2211. Medicare sequestration\nSection 251A(6)(D) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 ( 2 U.S.C. 901a(6)(D) ) is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin clause (i), by striking 8 months and inserting 10 months ; and\n**(2)**\nin clause (ii), by striking 4 months and inserting 2 months .\nIII\nHuman services\n#### 2301. Sexual risk avoidance education extension\nSection 510 of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 710 ) is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin subsection (a)(1)\u2014\n**(A)**\nby striking the period beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting fiscal year 2025 ; and\n**(B)**\nby striking or 2025 ; and\n**(2)**\nin subsection (f)(1), by striking the period beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025, an amount equal to the pro rata portion of the amount appropriated for the corresponding period for and inserting for fiscal year 2025, an amount equal to the amount appropriated for .\n#### 2302. Personal responsibility education extension\nSection 513 of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 713 ) is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin subsection (a)(1)\u2014\n**(A)**\nin subparagraph (A), in the matter preceding clause (i), by striking the period beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting fiscal year 2025 ; and\n**(B)**\nin subparagraph (B)(i), by striking the period beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025 and inserting fiscal year 2025 ; and\n**(2)**\nin subsection (f), by striking the period beginning on October 1, 2024, and ending on March 31, 2025, an amount equal to the pro rata portion of the amount appropriated for the corresponding period and inserting fiscal year 2025, an amount equal to the amount appropriated for fiscal year 2024 .\n#### 2303. Extension of funding for family-to-family health information centers\nSection 501(c)(1)(A)(viii) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 701(c)(1)(A)(viii) ) is amended by striking $3,000,000 for the portion of fiscal year 2025 before April 1, 2025. and inserting $6,000,000 for fiscal year 2025 .\nIV\nMedicaid\n#### 2401. Delaying Medicaid DSH reductions\nSection 1923(f)(7)(A) of the Social Security Act ( 42 U.S.C. 1396r\u20134(f)(7)(A) ) is amended\u2014\n**(1)**\nin clause (i)\u2014\n**(A)**\nin the matter preceding subclause (I)\u2014\n**(i)**\nby striking For the period beginning April 1, 2025, and ending September 30, 2025, and for and inserting For ; and\n**(ii)**\nby striking through 2027 and inserting through 2028 ;\n**(B)**\nin subclause (I), by striking or period ; and\n**(C)**\nin subclause (II), by striking or period ; and\n**(2)**\nin clause (ii)\u2014\n**(A)**\nby striking for the period beginning April 1, 2025, and ending September 30, 2025, and ; and\n**(B)**\nby striking through 2027 and inserting through 2028 .\nC\nOther Matters\n#### 3101. Commodity futures trading commission whistleblower program\nSection 1(b) of Public Law 117\u201325 (135 Stat. 297; 136 Stat. 2133; 136 Stat. 5984) is amended in each of paragraphs (3) and (4) by striking March 14, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 3102. Protection of certain facilities and assets from unmanned aircraft\nSection 210G(i) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 ( 6 U.S.C. 124n(i) ) is amended by striking March 14, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 3103. Additional special assessment\nSection 3014 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by striking March 14, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 3104. National cybersecurity protection system authorization\nSection 227(a) of the Federal Cybersecurity Enhancement Act of 2015 ( 6 U.S.C. 1525(a) ) is amended by striking March 14, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 3105. Extension of temporary order for fentanyl-related substances\nEffective as if included in the enactment of the Temporary Reauthorization and Study of the Emergency Scheduling of Fentanyl Analogues Act ( Public Law 116\u2013114 ), section 2 of such Act is amended by striking March 31, 2025 and inserting September 30, 2025 .\n#### 3106. Budgetary effects\n##### (a) Statutory PAYGO scorecards\nThe budgetary effects of divisions B and C shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.\n##### (b) Senate PAYGO scorecards\nThe budgetary effects of divisions B and C shall not be entered on any PAYGO scorecard maintained for purposes of section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress).\n##### (c) Classification of budgetary effects\nNotwithstanding Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines set forth in the joint explanatory statement of the committee of conference accompanying Conference Report 105\u2013217 and section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, the budgetary effects of divisions B and C shall not be estimated\u2014\n**(1)**\nfor purposes of section 251 of such Act;\n**(2)**\nfor purposes of an allocation to the Committee on Appropriations pursuant to section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974; and\n**(3)**\nfor purposes of paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 as being included in an appropriation Act.",
```
